methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $575M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Kinova Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Late Stage)

data.narrative:
VulcanForms is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by John Hart and Martin Feldmann that has raised $575M+ to date. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector comprises nine peer companies tracked in available data: one public entity and eight private firms including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity. A narrow peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the sector—the dataset identified zero peers meeting strict stage and valuation-tier filters, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold required for reliable median triangulation.

The stage-progression method represents the sole applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised ($575M) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $690M. This method is defensible given VulcanForms' demonstrated ability to raise substantial institutional capital, though it relies on the assumption that stage-based step-ups accurately reflect this company's specific risk-return profile.

The triangulated valuation range derived from this single-method analysis spans $586M (low) to $793M (high), with a median of $690M. This range reflects modest sensitivity bounds rather than deep cross-method validation. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer comparables to benchmark revenue multiples or growth rates, and the reliance on a single methodology. Additional data on revenue trajectory, margins, or market share would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
